channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Cease-Fire
item.description: Trump extended the Israel and Lebanon cease-fire for three more weeks to advance a peace agreement in Iran. The president said the truce in Lebanon would last three more weeks.

item.title: EU formally approves 90bn euro Ukraine loan and new sanctions on Russia
item.description: The EU approved a 90bn euro loan to Ukraine and new Russia sanctions. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomes the development, urging that the first tranche be disbursed by May or June.

item.title: Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran
item.description: Donald Trump said he is keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran. Donald Trump said he is the one keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran. Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran.

item.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
item.description: The US intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran. Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

item.title: Iran dismisses Trump’s claim of leadership rift, says nation is ‘one soul’
item.description: Iranian officials denied a leadership rift, citing the nation's "iron unity". Several Iranian officials share same message, emphasising what they call the 'iron unity of the nation and government'. Iran dismisses Trump’s claim of leadership rift, says nation is ‘one soul’.

item.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
item.description: Afghan refugees who helped US forces may be sent to Congo. Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U. S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or returning home to live under the Taliban.

item.title: What is Europe's plan to secure the Strait of Hormuz?
item.description: France and the UK are leading a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. France and the UK are leading efforts to establish a multinational mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Mysterious Deaths, Disappearances Rattle US, China
item.description: At least nine scientists have died under mysterious circumstances, reports from Chinese and overseas media indicate. The cause of these deaths and several disappearances is not specified. Reports from Chinese and overseas media point to at least nine deaths of scientists.

item.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
item.description: European airlines are canceling tens of thousands of flights due to soaring jet fuel costs amid the Iran war. Energy authorities warn of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished.

item.title: Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks
item.description: Trump will meet Lebanon and Israel envoys during peace talks after a 10-day truce was announced. The United States announced a 10-day truce shortly after the first meeting. Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks.

item.title: Mojtaba Injured, A 'Board' Of Generals Helps Take The Calls In Iran
item.description: Iran's military establishment took control after Mojtaba was injured. The balance of power appears firmly tilted toward the military establishment. Mojtaba Injured, A 'Board' Of Generals Helps Take The Calls In Iran.

item.title: Air Defence System Activated In Tehran Amid Reports Of Explosions
item.description: Tehran activated its air defense system after reports of explosions in several parts of the capital. The Mehr news agency confirmed the activation. Mehr news agency reported that the systems were activated in several parts of the capital.

item.title: Crash That Killed 2 Americans Is a Reminder of the Depth of Security Ties in Mexico
item.description: A crash in northern Mexico killed two CIA officers, sparking questions about authorization and sovereignty. The incident reflects a longstanding security partnership between the US and Mexico. The deaths of two C. I. A.

item.title: Another US Aircraft Carrier Reaches Gulf, Joins 2 Already Operating There
item.description: A US aircraft carrier has joined two already operating in the Gulf, amid rising tensions with Iran. The deployment was reported in a live update on US-Iran war news. US-Iran War News Live Update:.

item.title: $106 Billion Loan Reflects E.U.’s View That Peace in Ukraine Is Far Away
item.description: The E. U. approved a $106 billion loan for Ukraine, heavily weighted toward military spending. This reflects the E. U.'s view that peace in Ukraine is unlikely in the near future. Unlike previous European assistance packages, this one is heavily weighted toward military spending, meant to put Ukraine on solid footing for a long fight.

item.title: Ceasefire extended: What's next in the Iran war?
item.description: The US and Iran have extended their ceasefire with no fixed deadline. Both countries are now seeking ways to protect their interests amid ongoing tensions. With no fixed ceasefire deadline for now, the US and Iran are looking for ways to safeguard their own interests.

item.title: Before Iran War, China Amassed World's Largest Oil Stockpile
item.description: China amassed the world's largest oil stockpile before the Iran war, a move likely aimed at securing its energy supply. China is the world's largest crude oil importer, making this stockpile crucial to its economy.

item.title: Blasts reported over Tehran, air defences activated
item.description: Blasts were reported over Tehran, prompting Iran to activate its air defenses. Iran had previously stated it wouldn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US blockades its ports.

item.title: Russia reports toxic rain, orders locals to stay home after Ukrainian oil strike
item.description: Russia reported toxic rain and ordered locals to stay home after a Ukrainian oil strike in Tuapse on April 20. The strike triggered a huge blaze and sent thick black smoke into the sky over the coastal city.

item.title: Dozens of Mexican mafia members arrested in California crackdown
item.description: Forty-three people were indicted on charges including murder and extortion in a California crackdown on the Mexican mafia.
